hypothesis: If a single score-based diffusion model trained only on metastable-state MD data is guided at inference by interpolated conditional scores via SBI and refined by SAA to maximize an approximate reaction coordinate while minimizing orthogonal directions, then it will sample transition-state configurations with committor probabilities near 0.5 and better alignment with true transition states across 2D potentials, alanine dipeptide, chignolin folding, and a chemical reaction, outperforming linear and geodesic interpolation baselines.
